story.title: Drag existing polygon points, insert points on edges, and add the keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+Z, Escape, Enter)
story.as_a: Frigate user refining a zone boundary
story.i_want: to grab any vertex of a closed polygon and drag it, click an edge to add a vertex, and use keyboard shortcuts while drawing
story.so_that: I can fix a slightly-off boundary in seconds instead of deleting and redrawing the whole polygon

acceptance_criteria: Given a closed polygon, when the cursor moves within 8px (display space) of a vertex, then the cursor changes to grab and the vertex highlights; when dragged, the vertex follows the cursor, the polygon redraws each frame, and YAML updates live during the drag

Given a closed polygon, when the user clicks within 6px of an edge segment (not near an existing vertex), then a new vertex is inserted at that position between the segment's endpoints and is immediately draggable
Given a drag ends (mouseup), when the new position is committed, then normalized coordinates are recomputed from the final pixel position and persistence fires once

Given an in-progress draft, when the user presses Ctrl/Cmd+Z, then the last draft point is removed; Escape clears the entire draft; Enter closes the draft polygon (same validation path as the Close button)
Given a vertex drag, when Ctrl/Cmd+Z is pressed after release, then the vertex returns to its pre-drag position (single-step move undo)

Given a polygon with more than 3 points, when the user double-clicks a vertex, then that vertex is deleted; double-click on a vertex of a 3-point polygon shows the min-points inline warning and does nothing
constraints: Raw Canvas API only — hit-testing via distance math (point: Euclidean ≤ 8px display; edge: point-to-segment distance ≤ 6px display), thresholds converted through the canvas display scale factor
Drag state lives in Store (Store.beginDrag(polyId, pointIndex), Store.dragTo(x,y), Store.endDrag()); View renders from state, never holds positions
Click-to-add-draft-point must not fire when the click hit an existing vertex or edge (hit-test priority: vertex > edge > new draft point)
YAML regeneration during drag throttled to animation frame; Persist debounce (spec 04) absorbs write frequency
Keyboard handlers ignore events when focus is in an input/textarea/select
edge_cases: Two vertices of different polygons overlapping — topmost (last-rendered) polygon's vertex wins the hit-test
Drag released outside the canvas bounds — position clamps to image bounds (0..W, 0..H), keeping normalized coords in 0–1
Drag started but Escape pressed mid-drag — drag cancels, vertex returns to origin position
Edge-insert click exactly on a shared vertex of two segments — treated as vertex hit, no insert
Touch devices — out of scope; mouse events only per desktop-first principle (document limitation)
risks: Medium: hit-test feel (too sticky/too slippery) needs tuning — thresholds defined in one constants block for fast iteration
Low: drag perf on 4K images — render loop already redraws whole canvas per frame in v0.1; acceptable at these sizes

open_questions.question: How should vertex delete be triggered?

options.label: Double-click vertex (Recommended)
options.info: No mode switch, discoverable, doesn't conflict with drag (which requires movement).

options.label: Right-click context menu
options.info: More discoverable but adds menu UI and intercepts browser context menu.

tasks.description: Implement hit-testing utilities (vertexAt, edgeAt with display-scale-aware thresholds) and rewire canvas mousedown/mousemove/mouseup from the single click handler
Implement Store drag actions (beginDrag/dragTo/endDrag with clamp + single-step undo snapshot) and edge-insert/vertex-delete actions
Implement keyboard layer: Ctrl+Z (draft point / last move), Escape (clear draft / cancel drag), Enter (close polygon), with input-focus guard
Add cursor/hover affordances (grab cursor, vertex highlight, edge midpoint hint) per existing color system
QA: drag accuracy vs YAML output, edge insert between every segment pair, clamp at image borders, shortcut matrix incl. focus guard, drag at non-1:1 display scales

docs_md:
## Visual Point Editing
Closed polygons become editable: hover a vertex to grab and drag it (YAML updates live), click an edge to insert a vertex, double-click a vertex to remove it (min 3 enforced). Keyboard: Ctrl+Z undoes the last draft point or last vertex move, Escape clears the draft or cancels a drag, Enter closes the current polygon. This spec also delivers the keyboard shortcuts the v0.1 doc claimed but never implemented.

stub_suggestions.purpose: One state machine instead of competing click handlers

metrics: Drag renders at ≥ 30fps on a 1080p image (rAF-throttled, measured via DevTools performance trace)
Vertex hit-test success within 8px display radius in 100% of QA grid positions, including at 50% display scale
Post-drag YAML coordinates match dragged pixel position within 0.001 normalized tolerance

decision_log.rationale: A mousedown/mousemove/mouseup state machine with hit-test priority (vertex > edge > draft point) is the standard pattern and the master spec explicitly allows raw Canvas only. Spec 01's normalized-canonical coords are the prerequisite that makes drag math a two-line conversion.
alternatives_considered: Rejected: adopt Fabric.js/Konva — explicitly prohibited unless raw canvas proves unmanageable; this interaction set does not justify it
Rejected: separate 'edit mode' toggle — modal UI for no benefit; hit-test priority disambiguates drawing vs editing naturally
Rejected: full undo/redo history — single-step move undo covers the Ctrl+Z expectation at a fraction of scope
tradeoffs: Overlapping vertices resolved by z-order, not a picker
decision_log.confidence_rationale: Logic is standard but interaction feel (thresholds, hover affordances) typically needs a tuning pass; flagged as the main iteration risk.

defaults_applied: Assuming vertex delete via double-click (oq_001 recommended option)
Assuming drag clamps to image bounds rather than allowing out-of-range coords (keeps Validate range rule theoretical)
Assuming mouse-only; touch deferred past v0.2 per desktop-first principle
